ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly states 'Hit'. Broad-shouldered blue-skinned humanoid in purple long coat in guarded fighting stance matches Hit's canonical design precisely. IP confirmed.

trivia_bullets.text: Hit is Universe 6's legendary assassin, known canonically as 'Never-Miss Hit' and the 'Legendary Hitman,' holding a perfect contract record across a career spanning over 1,000 years. He was introduced during the God of Destruction Champa Arc as the strongest fighter in Universe 6's tournament roster. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Hit, via WebSearch snippet]

Hit's signature technique, Time-Skip, does not stop time outright but creates a momentary pocket dimension storing frozen time, allowing Hit to move freely and strike during a tenth-of-a-second window. As Hit battles stronger opponents, Time-Skip evolves: the window expands (up to half a second) and he gains the ability to encase opponents in a 'time cage,' suspending their movements. [Dragon Ball Official Site: Weekly Character Showcase no. 103 — Hit, title confirmed via WebSearch snippet]

BT1-003 is an Uncommon Blue Battle card in the U6 cohort block. It is numerically adjacent to BT1-002 (Vados) in the set, grouping the U6 fighters together. No additional card-effect text was retrievable from available sources for this specific printing.

Hit's design deliberately inverts the typical anime fighter aesthetic: he is stocky and compact rather than lean, expressive, or dramatic. His stoicism and economy of movement are design cues that signal the assassin-not-warrior archetype. Community discourse on r/dragonball notes the contrast with Universe 7's fighters as a deliberate visual tension. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Hit character page, via WebSearch snippet]
